title: Margin Signal Field
prompt:
Create a production-ready Margin Signal Field using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original background treatment uses a quiet white canvas with a slim margin rail, a few neutral coordinates, and one selectable coral signal point. Selecting a point updates a small readable caption while preserving the background as a supporting surface rather than a distracting illustration. Use a very light #F4F5F6 stage, white or near-white canvas, #ECECEE rules, #111111 labels, and #4A4A4F support copy. Use coral as the only bright accent and never put a gradient on the card.

Implement the selectable coordinates as real 44px buttons over a relative canvas. They need explicit names, aria-pressed, visible focus, and a polite live status. Click, Enter, and Space move the active signal. Use a stiffness 300, damping 26 spring for the active ring and a 160ms fade for the caption; decorative lines should be aria-hidden. Keep state local and do not use timers, random coordinates, browser storage, network requests, remote content, or fixed page-level positioning.

At 320px, leave sufficient space between targets and keep the caption legible. At larger widths, allow the field to breathe naturally in the detail preview. Use semantic heading structure, logical tab order, AA contrast, and textual state rather than color alone. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by positioning the ring without travel. Return a complete client component plus a pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML that can run without JavaScript.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(1),p=[['North','24%','28%'],['Center','52%','54%'],['South','76%','72%']];return
